key_insight: Optimization can be viewed as a coupled geometrodynamic system where a time-varying metric changes conditioning and transport, but cannot eliminate fundamental critical-point or convexity obstructions.
engineering_relevance: The framework gives adaptive-optimizer designers a benchmark for what geometric adaptation can and cannot improve, helping separate real preconditioning gains from impossible gains due to invariants.
method: Defines optimization geometrodynamics with parameter motion, particle distribution transport, and controlled Riemannian metric evolution.
Introduces dynamic geometric complexity as the minimum geometric cost needed to reduce an optimization difficulty observable.
Analyzes oracle benchmark cases including Hessian-matching flows, spectral Onsager relaxation, discrete exponential projection updates, and gauge-invariant observables.
evidence_signal: The paper is theory-only and reports formal theorems and proofs rather than empirical experiments or datasets, using the strongly convex quadratic oracle model as its benchmark setting.
watch_item: Practical use depends on specifying admissible metric families, accurate curvature estimates, and controlling discretization error, which may limit direct implementation.
paper_title: Optimization Geometrodynamics: A Framework for Dynamic Geometric Optimization
